name: PR4 Conhal Trails
name_original: Trilhos do Conhal (PR 4) — Monumento Natural das Portas de Ródão
description: Hike the moderate PR4 Conhal Trails for 6.8 miles (10.9 km) through diverse landscapes and Roman gold mine remnants in Portugal.
region_intro: Hiking around Santana, located on the northern coast of Madeira Island, offers diverse landscapes ranging from ancient laurel forests to dramatic coastal cliffs. The region is characterized by verdant mountains, deep valleys, and the unique Laurissilva Forest, a UNESCO World Heritage site. Hikers can explore an extensive network of trails that traverse this UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. Best hiking trails around Santana The most popular hiking route is PR4 Conhal Trails, a 6.7 miles (10.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 9 minutes to complete. This moderate route features varied terrain with an elevation gain of 255 meters. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castelo de Ródão – View of the Tagus River Valley loop from Ródão, a moderate 6.6 miles (10.7 km) path. This trail offers views of the Tagus River Valley and includes an ascent of 355 meters. Local hikers also love the Ilha do Cabecinho – Pego das Portas de Ródão loop from Santana, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 16 minutes. Hiking around Santana is defined by ancient laurel forests, verdant mountains, and dramatic coastal views. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy and moderate trails. The routes in Santana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 60 hikers have used komoot to explore Santana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Santana?
A: Santana offers a diverse network of trails for hikers. Our guide features 4 curated hiking routes, ranging from easy strolls to more challenging paths, allowing you to explore the region's unique landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain and difficulty can I expect on Santana's hiking trails?
A: The trails around Santana feature varied terrain, from ancient laurel forests to dramatic coastal cliffs and verdant mountains. You'll find a mix of easy and moderate routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2242396720/pr4-conhal-trails" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PR4 Conhal Trails&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route with an elevation gain of 255 meters, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32160607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Vila Velha de Ródão&lt;/a&gt; is an easier option with less elevation.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Santana?
A: Yes, Santana offers trails suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32160607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Vila Velha de Ródão&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route, perfect for a family outing. The region's natural beauty, including the Laurissilva Forest, provides an engaging environment for all ages.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Santana is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the ancient Laurissilva Forest, a UNESCO World Heritage site, and enjoy dramatic coastal views. Along some routes, you might encounter highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6478615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pego das Portas de Ródão&lt;/a&gt;, a notable natural monument. The region is also known for its traditional Santana Houses, which add to the cultural charm.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Santana?
A: The hiking routes in Santana are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from lush forests to stunning viewpoints, and the well-maintained paths that make exploring the region a rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Santana?
A: Yes, there are circular routes to enjoy. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10369913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castelo de Ródão – View of the Tagus River Valley loop from Ródão&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate circular path with views of the Tagus River Valley. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21949790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ilha do Cabecinho – Pego das Portas de Ródão loop from Santana&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through varied landscapes.

Q: Is it possible to find cafes or facilities near the hiking trails in Santana?
A: While specific cafes directly on every trail might be limited, the Parque Florestal das Queimadas, a popular starting point for many levada walks, offers facilities and is a good spot to relax. For more general information on points of interest and facilities in Santana, you can visit &lt;a href="https://visitmadeira.com/en/where-to-go/madeira/points-of-interest-in-santana/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Visit Madeira's Santana page&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Santana?
A: Santana, located on Madeira's northern coast, offers pleasant hiking conditions year-round due to its mild climate. However, for the most comfortable experience, spring (April-June) and autumn (September-November) are often ideal, with moderate temperatures and lush scenery. Winter can bring more rain, especially in higher elevations, but the Laurissilva Forest remains beautiful.

Q: Are there any specific attractions or points of interest to explore in Santana besides hiking?
A: Beyond the trails, Santana is known for its iconic triangular-shaped, thatched-roof Traditional Santana Houses (Casas Típicas de Santana), which are a must-see cultural attraction. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6478643" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Conhal Interpretive Center&lt;/a&gt; for insights into the local history and environment. For more information on attractions, visit &lt;a href="https://visitmadeira.com/en/where-to-go/madeira/north-coast/santana/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Visit Madeira's Santana guide&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How long do hikes near Santana typically take?
A: Hiking durations vary depending on the route's length and difficulty. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2242396720/pr4-conhal-trails" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PR4 Conhal Trails&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 3 hours and 9 minutes to complete, while the shorter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32160607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Vila Velha de Ródão&lt;/a&gt; can be finished in about 1 hour and 10 minutes.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Santana?
A: Santana is a well-known destination on Madeira, and local bus services connect various parts of the island. While specific routes to every trailhead might require planning, major starting points like Parque Florestal das Queimadas are generally accessible. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for the most up-to-date information on routes and timings.
tips: Pego das Portas is a charming place located in the Naturtejo Geopark, in the municipality of Nisa, in Portugal, with a privileged view of the Portas de Ródão natural monument and an excellent place for birdwatching (griffins).
The Conhal do Arneiro Roman Gold Mine is a gigantic mine that resulted from the gravitational dismantling of sedimentary deposits in the Tagus Valley by hydraulic action.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the PR4 Conhal Trails?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route that passes through the charming village of Arneiro. You can typically find parking within or near the village, which serves as a convenient starting point for the hike.

faqs.question: How difficult is the PR4 Conhal Trails route?
faqs.answer: The PR4 Conhal Trails are generally classified as having a moderate difficulty level. While some sections might be considered medium/low, the estimated duration of around 4 hours and 30 minutes for the 10.8 km route suggests a reasonable level of fitness is beneficial.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best seasons for hiking this trail are spring and autumn. The summer heat in the region can make the journey considerably more challenging, so it's advisable to avoid the hottest months.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the PR4 Conhal Trails?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally dog-friendly, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially since the route passes through natural areas and agricultural land. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike the PR4 Conhal Trails or visit the Monumento Natural das Portas de Ródão?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the PR4 Conhal Trails or to visit the Monumento Natural das Portas de Ródão. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The path is primarily composed of hard-packed dirt and rocks, along with gravel-strewn sections and quartzite walls. You'll also traverse ancient olive tree terraces and pass by traditional small vegetable gardens.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the PR4 Conhal Trails?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a rich blend of natural beauty and historical sites. Key highlights include the impressive Viewpoint of Portas de Ródão, the geological formation of Pego das Portas de Ródão, and the fascinating Roman Gold Mine of Conhal do Arneiro with its gigantic pebble piles. You'll also pass through the charming village of Arneiro.

faqs.question: Is the trail well-marked?
faqs.answer: Yes, the PR4 Conhal Trails are known for being very well-marked, making it easy for hikers to navigate without getting lost. Look for the official trail markers along the route.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The area is significant for birdwatching, particularly around the Portas de Ródão. You may have opportunities to observe griffons in silent flight, as well as various aquatic birds near the Tagus River. The diverse vegetation also supports a range of smaller wildlife.

faqs.question: Are there any detours or extended options available?
faqs.answer: Yes, hikers can take a short detour to visit the old iron mine known as Buraca da Faiopa, though caution is advised. Additionally, an extended version combining PR4 and PR9 covers approximately 14 km and is considered more physically demanding if you're looking for a longer challenge.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, we recommend bringing sturdy hiking shoes, sufficient water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and snacks. Binoculars would be great for birdwatching at the viewpoints. A map or GPS device is always a good idea, even with a well-marked trail.

waypoints.name: Roman Gold Mine of Conhal do Arneiro
